# Contributing to Awesome Claude

Thanks for helping make this list better! Contributions of all sizes are welcome — a single great link is a perfectly good pull request.

## What belongs here

**Awesome Claude** curates the best resources for [Anthropic's Claude](https://claude.ai): official products and docs, the Claude Code ecosystem, the Model Context Protocol (MCP), SDKs and frameworks, learning material, and notable tools and projects built with Claude.

A resource is a good fit if it is:

- **Relevant** — directly about Claude, or with first-class Claude/Anthropic support.
- **High quality** — actively maintained, reputable, and genuinely useful.
- **Not redundant** — adds something the list doesn't already cover.

## How to add something

1. **Fork** this repository and create a branch.
2. Add your link to the most fitting section of [`README.md`](../README.md), keeping entries **alphabetically ordered** within their subsection where practical.
3. Use this exact format — one line, a real HTTPS link, an em dash, and a short factual description:
   ```
   - [Name](https://example.com) — One concise line describing what it is and why it's useful.
   ```
4. Keep descriptions **neutral and factual** — no marketing language, no star counts (they go stale).
5. Make sure the **link works** and points to the canonical source (prefer the project's homepage or its GitHub repo).
6. If you add a whole new category, update the **Table of Contents** too.
7. Open a pull request with a clear title. One logical change per PR is easiest to review.

## Translations

Translations live in [`translations/`](../translations). To add or update one, mirror the structure of the main `README.md`. Feel free to open a PR that only touches a translation.

## Removing links

If a resource is abandoned, broken, or no longer relevant, a PR that removes it is just as valuable as one that adds a link. Please note why in the PR description.

## Quality bar

Maintainers may decline or ask to revise entries that are off-topic, low quality, duplicated, or self-promotional without clear value. Nothing personal — the goal is to keep the list worth reading.
